tldr: Two punch-list items shipped this run (#17, #31) plus a wave of non-audit improvements. #17 closed G16 — but with a revised shape: instead of a separate /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page (the original spec), PR #78 renamed the parent /services/medical-acupuncture/ service to 'Acupuncture & Dry Needling' across nav, homepage card, JSON-LD, breadcrumbs and inter-service cards; Min Jin is positioned as the TCM lead, Nina Hu as the Western medical acupuncture / dry needling lead; new FAQ entries cover acupuncture-vs-dry-needling, which practitioner to book, and what to expect at a first dry-needling session. #31 closed G24 — PR #77 rewrote every condition AND every service intro to open with a 40–60 word citation-ready direct answer ('Back pain treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch combines …'), then keeps the existing evidence-informed prose as the second paragraph. Non-audit improvements: PRs #80/#81 added per-practitioner booking pages with Cliniko pre-targeted (each practitioner gets their own /book/<slug>/ URL), homepage practitioner cards now point at those specific pages, and PR #82 distinguished Min's ACC eligibility (registered to treat, doesn't lodge claims) from Nina's (registered osteopath who lodges claims and integrates dry needling within ACC-subsidised consultations). PRs #84-#93 covered Kaylee Frost's team photo (multiple iterations + colour correction + optimisation) and PR #91 filled the /admin Decap CMS coverage gaps so all editable content blocks now have CMS controls.

Search visibility: 38 queries re-checked against live SERPs on 2026-05-22. **First-ever Meridian ranking in any tracked top 10 — meridianosteopathy.co.nz now sits at position 5 for 'osteopath vs physiotherapist christchurch'** (non-aggregator rank, between christchurchosteopath.co.nz and 13thbeachhealthservices.com.au). Almost certainly Google blending our homepage into the comparison-intent query rather than a content-led win — we don't have a dedicated /osteopath-vs-physio/ page, but the homepage's broadened title (now 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture, Dry Needling & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch' after PR #78) plus the apex-domain consolidation from PR #73 and the citation-ready intros from #31/#77 are starting to register. Priority preserved at 30 per the 4-10 rank rule. Every other tracked query stayed absent from top 10, so the standard -10 decay applies to the remaining 37. Notable shifts vs 2026-05-15: chchacupuncture.co.nz reclaimed #1 for 'acupuncture christchurch' (was outside top 5 last week, thehouseofacupuncture had held #1); 'osteopath hillmorton' is STILL all-UK results (winchmore-osteopaths.co.uk, bostonosteopathichealth.com, hillsosteopath.com, fionarobertson.net) 12 days after item #29 added Hillmorton to flowing intro copy — indexing lag continues; 'osteopath wigram' and 'osteopath addington' also remain hollow on the NZ side. Two new queries got first-time checks this run ('whiplash osteopath christchurch', 'concussion osteopath christchurch') — both currently dominated by Triskelion-family clinics (triskelionosteopathy.co.nz, triskelionconcussioncare.co.nz) plus Better Health and osteo.co.nz/concussion/. Three new derived queries added to the pool ('post concussion osteopath christchurch', 'lower back pain christchurch', 'tennis elbow osteopath christchurch') — last one tracks Nina's recently-added What I Treat tag.

Backlog after this run: 7 items pending (#12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #25, #32). Tier 1 → none. Tier 2 → #12, #13, #25, #32. Tier 3 → #14, #15, #16. One new item added (#32 — /conditions/concussion/ page mirroring the existing condition-page pattern; triskelionconcussioncare.co.nz + osteo.co.nz/concussion/ + triskelionosteopathy.co.nz/concussion/ own that SERP, HeadWise and Better Health also surface). Closed gaps: G16 (Acupuncture & Dry Needling rename gives us an indexable URL targeting Western dry-needling intent), G24 (citation-ready intros shipped). Remaining gaps: G7 (no ACC landing — under #25), G8 (no blog — under #14), G15 (no Reddit citations — under #15). Industry signal this week: AI Overviews now trigger on 88% of healthcare queries (up from 72% in 2024) per BrightEdge / Healthcare Success May-2026 data, and ClaudeBot crawl volume grew 800% at the start of 2026 — our explicit allow-list in robots.txt and the now-shipping citation-ready intros position us to benefit if/when our condition pages get indexed.
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images
Site-wide sweep removing 'GP referral' from patient-facing copy (per the CLAUDE.md copy rule); condition CTA banners simplified to heading + booking button only

Google Business Profile services audit completed manually (item #21) — owner added Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine to GBP service list

/conditions/neck-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, mirroring the four item-#10 pages; covers acute vs chronic, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links back to the osteopathy service (item #22, PR #63)

/conditions/back-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema; covers acute vs chronic, sciatica overlap, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links to /conditions/sciatica/ and the osteopathy service (item #23, PR #64)

<link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown" href="/llms.txt"> + the same for /llms-full.txt added in the HTML head — signals AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists alongside the HTML (item #26, PR #62)

Physician schema upgraded to Schema.org v30 IndividualPhysician on every team profile, with practicesAt: { @id: '#clinic' } linking each practitioner to the MedicalClinic node — sharpens entity disambiguation for ChatGPT / Perplexity / AI Overviews (item #27, PR #65)

Image alt text rewritten descriptive-not-titular across team profiles (team-profile.njk now uses member.photoAlt with name fallback), condition pages, and service pages — each now describes what's visible plus relevant clinical context. Closes G14 (item #28, PR #70 / bundled in #71)

Hillmorton + Wigram + Addington + Halswell + Cashmere now appear in flowing intro copy on every service page in services.json (osteopathy, acupuncture, herbal medicine), not just the site-wide footer line. Closes G22 (item #29, PR #68 / bundled in #71)

'Conditions we commonly treat' block added to the osteopathy service page linking all six condition pages (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery), plus a matching 'Common conditions we treat' section on the homepage with high-intent button links — gives the condition pages an internal-link surface beyond the nav dropdown so Google can pass relevance signals to them. Closes G23 (item #30, PR #69 / bundled in #71)

/book/ page made AI-citable — added ReserveAction schema and visible intro content so ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini can recommend it as a booking action, not just an interstitial. Especially relevant after Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with EHR integration, since AI assistants are increasingly handling the appointment-booking step (PR #72)

Explicit www → apex 301 redirects added in netlify.toml — Google now consolidates link equity onto the canonical meridianosteopathy.co.nz apex domain rather than splitting it between www and apex variants (PR #73)

/services/medical-acupuncture/ renamed to 'Acupuncture & Dry Needling' across nav, homepage card, JSON-LD, breadcrumbs and inter-service cards; Min Jin positioned as TCM lead and Nina Hu as Western medical acupuncture / dry needling lead; new FAQ entries on acupuncture-vs-dry-needling, which practitioner to book, and what to expect at a first dry-needling session. Implementation diverged from the original spec (which had been a separate /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page) — the rename achieves the same G16 goal of one indexable URL targeting Western dry-needling intent, without splitting authority across two pages. Closes G16 (item #17, PR #78)

Every condition page (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery) and every service page (osteopathy, acupuncture, herbal-medicine) now opens with a 40–60 word citation-ready direct answer in the 'X treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch …' format, followed by the existing evidence-informed prose as the second paragraph onward. The answer-leading sentence also becomes the first sentence of the MedicalCondition / Service JSON-LD description field, so it propagates to AI-citation crawlers. Closes G24 (item #31, PR #77)

Per-practitioner booking pages added — each practitioner now has their own /book/<slug>/ URL with Cliniko pre-targeted to their calendar (no more landing on the generic 'select a practitioner' page after a homepage card click); homepage practitioner cards repointed at the matching per-practitioner /book/ page. Reduces clicks from 'pick a practitioner' to 'pick a time' from 3 to 1 (PRs #80, #81)

Min Jin's ACC eligibility correctly distinguished from Nina Hu's across acupuncture pages and per-practitioner booking pages — Nina is an ACC-registered osteopath who lodges claims in clinic and integrates dry needling within ACC-subsidised consultations; Min is registered to treat as an ACC acupuncture provider but does not lodge new claims herself. Wording on /book/min-jin/ adjusted accordingly (PR #82)
Kaylee Frost team photo updated, colour-corrected, and optimised across multiple iterations (PRs #86, #88, #90, #93)
/admin Decap CMS coverage gaps filled — all editable content blocks on the live site now have corresponding CMS controls so the clinic can self-serve copy updates without code changes (PR #91)

gaps.title: No ACC landing page
gaps.why: Neck-pain and back-pain condition pages now ship (items #22, #23 closed). The remaining piece of G7 is a single indexable /acc/ URL — osteo.co.nz still owns 'osteopath acc christchurch' and we have ACC mentions scattered across the site but no canonical landing. Reinforced 2026-05-22: thehouseofacupuncture.co.nz/our-services/acc-acupuncture-chistchurch still owns 'acupuncture acc christchurch' for the same structural reason. Scoped under item #25.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months. April-2026 industry data: 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within 30 days, 50% of Perplexity citations come from content less than 13 weeks old. osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine/cranial/baby-osteo queries on blog-post velocity — 1 short post per condition is enough to claim those niches. MK Osteopathy is doing the same for TMJ. May-2026 BrightEdge / Healthcare Success data confirms the pattern is hardening: AI Overviews now trigger on 88% of healthcare queries (up from 72% in 2024), with cited brands earning 35% more clicks and AI visitors converting 4.4× higher than organic. Reinforced 2026-05-22: osteopath-christchurch.com is still publishing fresh dated posts (most recent: 'Cranial Osteopathy for Fatigue and Recovery in Older Adults' dated 2026-04-02) and continues to surface across cranial / migraine / baby / arthritis / stress queries.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit appears in ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations (May 2026 CMSWire/Conductor data). Reddit is the second most visible website in Google search results in 2026 (only Wikipedia ahead) and is now the most-cited domain across all major AI models. Tinuiti's Q1 2026 AI Citations Trends Report shows social-media share of AI citations climbed past 9% Oct-2025 → Jan-2026, with Reddit accounting for the dominant share of that growth across nine product categories. ClaudeBot crawl volume grew 800% at the start of 2026 as Anthropic scaled its web search API — Reddit citations are increasingly the path into Claude's answers too, not just Perplexity's. Item #15 still applies.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live. With the citation-ready 40–60 word answers now in place (item #31 shipped 2026-05-17), wrapping them in a speakable block is the natural follow-up — gives voice assistants a single span to read aloud rather than guessing.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache. Reinforced this week: post-March-2026 Google update weights review recency more heavily than raw count; GBP is the primary data source for AI-driven local search results per May-2026 PinMeTo / MedRankSEO data.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Ahrefs analysis of 17M AI citations (April 2026) found AI-cited content is 25.7% fresher than traditional Google organic results — 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within the last 30 days. Freshness compounds. Confirmed again 2026-05-22: osteopath-christchurch.com still owns headaches/cranial/baby/teeth-grinding/stress/arthritis queries on the strength of one short blog post per condition (most recent post dated 2026-04-02); MK Osteopathy still owns the TMJ slot the same way. Per BrightEdge May-2026 data, AI Overviews now trigger on 88% of healthcare queries (up from 72% in 2024) — the freshness signal feeds directly into AI Overview citation eligibility. With /admin CMS coverage now filled (PR #91), the editorial path is shorter than it was — adding a Decap blog collection would let the clinic publish without code changes.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. Reddit is now ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations across major AI platforms (May 2026 Conductor / CMSWire data) and the second most visible site in Google results overall (Wikipedia first). Tinuiti Q1 2026 data: social-media share of AI citations climbed past 9% Oct-2025 → Jan-2026 with Reddit accounting for the dominant share of that growth. ClaudeBot scaled 800% at start of 2026 — Reddit is increasingly the path into Claude's answers too. 90/10 rule: 90% non-promotional, 2–4 weeks of karma-building before any mention of Meridian.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old. Reinforced 2026-05-02: Google's March 2026 core update now weights review recency AND owner response rate more heavily than raw count. Healthcare best-practice = at least 1 new review/week. May-2026 PinMeTo data: GBP is the primary data source for AI-driven local search across Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT and Perplexity location queries.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query (confirmed again 2026-05-22). We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Same gap reinforces 'acupuncture acc christchurch' (thehouseofacupuncture.co.nz/our-services/acc-acupuncture-chistchurch owns that one for the same structural reason). Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, who lodges the claim (Nina, our osteopath; Min for acupuncture-specific claims), how to lodge, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.

items.title: Add /conditions/concussion/ page — Triskelion Concussion Care owns 'concussion osteopath christchurch'
items.body: First check 2026-05-22 of 'concussion osteopath christchurch' shows triskelionconcussioncare.co.nz at #1, osteo.co.nz/concussion/ at #2, triskelionosteopathy.co.nz/concussion/ at #3; HeadWise (headwise.co.nz/who-we-are/andy-gibson) and Better Health (anastasia-mcpherson-osteopath-christchurch) also rank in the top 10, and Russell Johns at Moorhouse Osteopathic Centre is named explicitly in the AI summary. Concussion is high-value, ACC-adjacent injury content (post-concussion symptoms — headaches, dizziness, fatigue, brain fog — overlap directly with the headache/migraine and neck pain conditions we already treat) and a single-condition specialist site (Triskelion Concussion Care) is currently the strongest competitor. The smallest fix is a /conditions/concussion/ page that mirrors the existing condition-page pattern (sciatica, neck-pain, back-pain, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery): MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, 40–60 word citation-ready intro (per #31's pattern), acute-vs-chronic / when-to-seek-urgent-care / ACC eligibility / how osteopathy fits / book-now sections. Add a corresponding entry to conditions.json and a tile in the homepage 'Common conditions we treat' grid. Page exists pattern to copy; effort S.
